title: Check LED polarity with a multimeter
description: Use a digital multimeter's diode mode to confirm a loose 5 mm LED's orientation before wiring. Keep power off, work on a dry surface, and wear safety goggles.

dialogue.text:
Lay the LED and meter on a dry, non-conductive surface and put on safety goggles.
We'll use diode-test mode to identify the positive lead.

Set the digital multimeter to diode mode and keep fingers behind the probe guards. Touch the red probe to the LED's long leg and the black to the short leg without forcing the leads or letting probes touch.
The LED should glow faintly or show about 1.8 V. Reverse the probes; it should stay dark or display OL. Stop if the leads heat up.

Nice! Now you know which lead is positive.
Turn off the meter, disconnect the probes, and store the LED for your next circuit.
